item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Okay, maybe I missed the proper time to speak up, but the link to 424 makes absolutely no sense at all.

  • 424 is a blob of grey goo that likes playing pranks
  • 1611 is an evil-spirit-thing-turned-"protector" that puts its master into a state worse than death

I don't understand the logic of the guy who suggested the link. Did he maybe make a typo on the scp number?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert changes at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got a chance to talk to Roget about his headcanon regarding this article. Apparently, the guardian of this guarded apartment operates and is motivated in a very similar manner as SCP-424, and the two phenomena maybe related (though it would be very difficult to tell given the entity in 1611 not being seen). As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

People(…)ook them and showed me. I didn't kno(…)anted, but he ripped them(…)wrapped my leg, it was still bleeding(…)help me please if you find this. I'm dropping it(…)find me please please ple

we can help. let us breathe life into you again

To this:

People(…)ook them and showed me. I didn't kno(…)anted, but he ripped them(…)wrapped my leg, it was still bleeding(…)help me please if you find this. I'm dropping it(…)find me please please ple

we can help. let us breathe life into you again

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is creepy in the most awesome, terrible way. I imagined EVERYTHING, from the diseased half dead woman crawling out the apartment to what the interior of a hotel room inhabited by a rotting near-corpse must look like. So disgusting and horrible. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As the subject approaches, it will move back into SCP-1611.

Had me at an upvote here, as this paragraph gave me one of the eeriest, visceral mental images I've had from reading here; but the addenda was a little too expository imo. Novote.

item.title: Image Takedown
item.content:encoded: SCP-1611's image was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact Ellie Mirra or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You basically summed up what I wanted to say. I believe the Foundation can be more creative with managing the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I think there's another entity like this on the site, except it fuels itself by hurting others. It acts pretty darn similar, but there's just one difference in the fundimental operation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good, especially the picture. I had trouble understanding the diary though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Spooky? Who names a file with the name spook-OH GOD."

It was something like that I was thinking before reading.

This one is good, but for some odd reason, with the image and with all the deal of the blurred face and damaged clothes, for me, looks more or less like The Ring thing.

Im not asuming anything, I just remember it after reading this.

Also, maybe you should check this:

In the event that SCP-1611 begins speaking, on-site sound dampening speakers are to be activated until it ceases.

Who is speaking, SCP-1611 or SCP-1611-1?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also got the Fifthist vibe from nothing in particular that I can put my finger on. Glad to know it's not just me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're close
Here's how it really went down

It's an entity which was originally a destructive, evil thing which underwent some kind of forced transformation. Maybe it was forced to change its behavior, maybe something broke its mind, or maybe it got a different mind put into it. Either way, it's now an entity trying to really help this woman, look out for her, but being incredibly ill-equipped to do so. Rather like a tank trying to help little old ladies across the street, and then being confused when it keeps reducing them to smears on the pavement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Furthermore, I sort of think

+ show block
– hide block

The reason the entity went so bad is because it started it bad. It's a destructive entity forced to act in a protective manner, so it acts protective the most destructive way it can as a sort of fuck you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You basically nailed it. I'm pleased as punch that someone got this on their own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, I get it.

+ show block
– hide block

The entity is sort of like the nanocloud, where it puts you in dangerous situations so it can protect you. That's why everything started to go wrong for her after. But then she got she got sick and started to die, possibly because of the amulet - the guy who gave it to her had 'all of the skin diseases', after all. So the entity 'protects' her by killing people and transplanting bones and organs and skin to the woman. It's gotten so bad she's not even really alive anymore, but it keeps luring in people to 'help' her. I'm a little unsure if it's a protection entity gone horribly wrong or something pretending to be a protection entity for shits and giggles, but either way it's pretty bad.

Is that more or less correct? Anyway, if it was just a stairway entity with a creepy luring technique I wouldn't like this, because we have one of those, but the story you can piece together from this makes it completely its own. Especially since while it does reveal a lot about its nature if you think hard, it certainly doesn't give everything. For example, I thought this had to do with the Fifthist Church at first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple but effective edit, improved article. Good work overall!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good point, I'll make the appropriate edits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one, but:

"11 people staying at the [REDACTED] hotel were killed over a 1-month period"

so either this thing is ambulatory outside the stairwell, or the Miami police department had nothing unusual to say about 11 people being murdered in the same hotel / hotel room? why didn't this hotel close down briefly over, say, 2 deaths?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this enough to upvote it, but I also have some qualms with it. The stairwell doesn't look particularly huge, for example, and the foundation isn't adverse to putting D-class into less than healthy situations, so what happens if someone takes a running jump down the stairs? Does SCP-1611-1 vanish into SCP-1611 with supernatural speed, or can you catch her?

That's a bit of a silly one but basically from the description as it stands, it seems like it'd be really easy to catch 1611-1 in some way, like throwing a net, and the fact that it's never brought up at all seems a bit… odd. It reads like the only way they've tried approaching the SCP is to slowly walk down the stairs towards it one by one; not by drilling in through a wall near it, not by jumping, not by running, not by throwing stuff down the stairs to catch her, not by sending a team down so 4 people can prop the door open while one looks inside etc…

Like I said, I like it enough to upvote it, but it really does feel more like a story than an SCP. The lack of a test log or anything similar to that really hurts the "foundation is taking care of this" aspect of it and makes it feel more like "some guys found this and wrote about it".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have earned my upvote. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've added some stuff to reconcile your confusion, namely adding the "ongoing tests" and fixing the dates to be how I originally intended.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The writing's solid, but it also feels like it's not done. Or, I guess, that the Foundation hasn't done enough. Why haven't they sent in unmanned drones to record things? Why haven't they attempted to establish communication with SCP-1611-1? What happens if you send down someone with instructions to not go past the threshold? What about the other Fridge Logic that other people have mentioned previously? You don't need to go into lots of detail on any of it, but even something like "testing is still underway" would make me like it a lot better.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That filename…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do like this, but it still seems as if it's just an idea that you thought was scary and then rather quickly made into an SCP.
I'd feel more drawn to this written out as some kind of creepypasta.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
People(…)ook them and showed me. I didn't kno(…)anted, but he ripped them(…)wrapped my leg, it was still bleeding(…)help me please if you find this. I'm dropping it(…)find me please please ple

we can help. let us breathe life into you again

Damn creepy, damn +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You wrote a decent SCP.

Well, I guess there's a first time for everything. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not as cool as Memetickemu, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm, so there's some entity that's trying to protect/help the woman, it lures people into the apartment to try to get them to help with her condition/ailment, the people don't know how to help, and… it uses (eventually fatal) torture to try to force them to help? It gets frustrated and takes its frustration out on them?

Any visual recording devices brought in by the subject will cease functioning, while audio recordings will be heavily distorted.

So the Foundation hasn't tried drilling spy-holes in the walls/floor/ceiling to see what's happening? Or using the laser-bounced-of-an-external-window trick to get audio?

The [REDACTED] hotel was condemned and converted to contain SCP-1611.

Then shouldn't the hotel be named Site-#?

EDIT: Also, if the hotel was condemned and then converted, wouldn't people notice people going in and out of the condemned building?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Diary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oops, missed that bit. Apologies. And is #2 about the creepypasta comment or the diary comment?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. The hotel bit is a stylistic choice.

2. That was intentional.

3.

The [REDACTED] hotel was condemned and converted to contain SCP-1611.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A bit too creepypasta for my tastes, and the second redaction in the final paragraph feels excessive. Why not just refer to it as "the hotel" once you've established you're redacting the name? Also, the damaged diary seems /too/ carefully damaged, and felt (to me) too obviously made up.

Further, the way it's written makes it seem like the Foundation left the hotel open, except for the room, which seems silly and incompetent of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first it reminded me a bit much of an episode of Doctor Who, but things got much better at the addendum. Certainly creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have mixed feelings about this. The image and the diary are creepy and unique, but the setup and the effects on people who enter the room make it feel a bit too much like "The Lodger" from Doctor Who.

EDIT: Eh, I guess the similarities aren't that much to the detriment of the SCP. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. The diary doesn't give away an obvious answer, but leaves some hint of a story behind this.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. It has just that right amount of creepy mixed in with the bizarre.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What is this?

I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I was posting this, there was an unfinished SCP draft already in the box

http://scpsandbox2.wikidot.com/unfinished-thing <—this thing.
